config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically take priority over individual rights, because that opens the door to abuse by governments or majorities claiming a "greater good." Individual rights are the basic protection that keeps society fair, especially for minorities and people with less power. In my view, a good society is one that balances the common good with strong protection for personal freedom, not one that sacrifices the individual whenever it is convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami penuh contoh ketika pemerintah atau mayoritas memakai alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” untuk membatasi kebebasan, dan itu justru sering merugikan minoritas. Hak individu harus menjadi batas yang kuat; kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa menginjak hak dasar orang. Kalau prioritasnya dibalik, kita terlalu mudah membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand why people emphasize harmony, order, and the common good, but I have also seen how easily “for society” can become a justification for silencing dissent, ignoring minorities, or letting officials overreach. The collective matters, but it should be pursued within firm limits that protect individual dignity and freedom. If individual rights are treated as secondary, society may become orderly on the surface while becoming unjust underneath.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa ketika negara atau mayoritas diberi terlalu banyak ruang atas nama “kebaikan bersama,” hasilnya sering justru penindasan terhadap minoritas dan pembatasan kebebasan yang semestinya dilindungi. Kepentingan sosial memang nyata, tetapi menurut saya ia harus dicapai lewat pembatasan yang ketat dan tidak boleh mengorbankan hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, berkeyakinan, dan perlindungan hukum. Kalau hak individu dianggap nomor dua, masyarakat mungkin terlihat tertib, tetapi mudah sekali menjadi tidak adil.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over individual rights, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “for the common good” can be used to silence critics or excuse unfair treatment. I do think social harmony and public order matter a lot, but they are only legitimate if they stay within clear limits that protect basic freedoms and minority rights. A society that ignores the individual may look stable for a while, yet it becomes fragile and unjust underneath. For me, the right balance is not choosing society over the person, but making sure society serves people without crushing them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara umum di atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, sejarah kami menunjukkan bahwa begitu “demi kebaikan bersama” dijadikan alasan utama, pemerintah dan mayoritas sering terlalu mudah melampaui batas dan merugikan minoritas. Hak individu bukan penghalang bagi masyarakat; justru itu pagar yang mencegah masyarakat menjadi alat penindasan. Kepentingan sosial penting, tetapi harus selalu dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, berkeyakinan, dan perlindungan hukum.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
